user_prompt: read `lib\Hotel\readme.md`, do not edit code yet, add to `lib\Hotel\readme.md` a list of state machine states if this is to be implemented as one state machine
agent_reply: Added the one-state-machine state list to [readme.md](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/readme.md). I did not edit `Hotel.h` or `Hotel.cpp`.
Added the one-state-machine state list to [readme.md](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/readme.md). I did not edit `Hotel.h` or `Hotel.cpp`.
Implemented `Hotel` as a namespace-backed power manager in [Hotel.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.h) and [Hotel.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.cpp).
What’s included:
- State machine from the README: blocked, active thresholds, light sleep states, shutdown.
- `pollCore0()` / `pollCore1()` synchronization before light sleep.
- User activity API, including ISR-safe activity notification.
- Blocking while recording, Bluetooth is active, Wi-Fi is active, or audio mode is active.
- Transition-only CPU/backlight changes with debug transition logging.
- `esp_timer_get_time()` wrapper for sleep-safe elapsed time.
- M5 PMIC shutdown, M5 light sleep when ready, timer-only ESP light sleep fallback before `M5.begin()`.
I also wired it into:
- [main.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp): both core loops call Hotel polling.
- [Buttons.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Buttons/Buttons.cpp): button activity resets Hotel timer.
- [FlyGui.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp): touch activity resets Hotel timer.

Verification: `pio run` still fails before compiling `Hotel` because of the existing `SdCard` namespace collision with SdFat in `src/mic_sd_recorder_demo.cpp`. The error is the same prior blocker: repo `namespace SdCard` conflicts with SdFat’s global `SdCard` typedef.

user_prompt: is `Hotel` disabled during audio playback? I have playback active and went grocery shopping, I come back, the playback is paused but it did not shutdown due to inactivity, I think Hotel should be allowed to dim the screen during playback, and also shutdown the device if the playback is paused
agent_reply: Yes, `Hotel` was effectively disabled during playback: `AudioManager::mode() != Stopped` made file playback look like “audio active,” which forced `Hotel::Blocked` and prevented dim/shutdown.
I changed it so:
- Recording and mic capture still fully block Hotel.
- Bluetooth and Wi-Fi still fully block Hotel.
- Active file playback can dim the screen after inactivity.
- Active file playback suppresses light sleep/shutdown while it is actually playing.
- Paused/stopped/finished playback no longer suppresses shutdown.
Files changed:
- [Hotel.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.cpp)
- [Hotel.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.h)
- [PlaybackView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WavPlayback/PlaybackView.cpp)
- [PlaybackView.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WavPlayback/PlaybackView.h)
- [Hotel/readme.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/readme.md)
Verified with:
`pio run -e thefly_sec1`
Build passed. `src/version.c` is still dirty separately from the build/version tooling; I did not manually edit it.

commit_message: hotel can shutdown if playback is paused
